title: Responsive CSS, Reflow, Progressive Enhancement, and Performance Budgets · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for responsive css, reflow, progressive enhancement, and performance budgets and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, parser, template, validator, host, CDN, DNS, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for responsive progressively enhanced performance budget. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practi…
objectives: Build intrinsic layouts with content-driven breakpoints, logical properties, flexible media, readable measures, and no fixed-viewport dependency.
Verify 320 CSS-pixel reflow, 200 and 400 percent zoom, text spacing, long tokens, code, tables, and translated content without two-dimensional page scrolling.
Deliver core reading and navigation in HTML, layer CSS and optional JavaScript, and define failure behavior for blocked or broken enhancements.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Mobile First Shell
steps.instruction:

content.text: A quiz publishing team handles case 4162 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate representative pages on reflow, progressive enhancement, and resource budgets; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Build intrinsic layouts with content-driven breakpoints, logical properties, flexible media, readable measures, and no fixed-viewport dependency.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Performance Evidence, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Performance Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Performance Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A quiz publishing team handles case 3448 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate representative pages on reflow, progressive enhancement, and resource budgets; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure cold and warm representative routes across mobile, tablet, desktop, slow networks, long content, missing cache, and disabled JavaScript.

For Static-site publishing and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Performance Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: A local desktop load time predicts field performance.

stimulus.title: Performance Evidence failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A local desktop load time predicts field performance.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure cold and warm representative routes across mobile, tablet, desktop, slow networks, long content, missing cache, and disabled JavaScript.

options.text: Accept the Performance Evidence result because visible responsive progressively enhanced performance budget output is the highest authority. That claim fails the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary for Performance Evidence.

For diagnose the hidden failure, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Performance Evidence for responsive progressively enhanced performance budget.
Run the Performance Evidence boundary case for responsive progressively enhanced performance budget, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Performance Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Performance Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz publishing team handles case 5067 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate representative pages on reflow, progressive enhancement, and resource budgets; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure cold and warm representative routes across mobile, tablet, desktop, slow networks, long content, missing cache, and disabled JavaScript.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Performance Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Performance Evidence through the concrete responsive progressively enhanced performance budget case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Performance Evidence, keep Performance Evidence connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Measure cold and warm representative routes across mobile, tablet, desktop, slow networks, long content, missing cache, and disabled JavaScript.
